title: newtonia aubrevillei subsp. lasiantha
intro: Newtonia aubrevillei subsp. lasiantha is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known for its medicinal and therapeutic properties. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds. Newtonia aubrevillei subsp. lasiantha has been reported to offer several health benefits, including the treatment of wounds, fever, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant are often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the affected area.
